text: The system shall support the bulk ingest of material and ingest of individual objects.
The system shall record all actions and decisions made concerning the submitted material in an audit trail.
The system shall assign a unique internal identifier (UID) to each digital object.
The system shall maintain the relationship between a parent object and any subsequent child objects.
The system shall support the use of multiple languages and non-Roman scripts for ingest, maintenance, and access.
The system shall perform virus checking on Submission Information Packages (SIPs).
The system shall validate the successful transfer of a SIP to a staging area using Cyclic Redundancy Checks (CRCs) or checksums.
The system shall provide a user-friendly GUI for NLM staff to perform manual quality control assurance on staged SIPs.
The system shall generate statistical and error reports for the quality assurance process.
The system shall transform SIPs into Archival Information Packages (AIPs) that conform to repository data formatting standards.
The system shall allow entry and validation of descriptive, administrative, and structural metadata.
The system shall store metadata in a database linked to corresponding objects via a unique identifier.
The system shall transfer AIPs to Archival Storage and provide a storage confirmation.
The system shall monitor and ensure that AIPs are not corrupted during internal transfers.
The system shall conform to HHS/NIH security standards and software security policies.
The system must be compatible with the NIH Consolidated Collocation Site (NCCS) off-site back-up facility.
The system shall provide disaster recovery capabilities including data backup and recovery.
The system shall perform routine and special data integrity checking using CRCs or checksums for each individual file.
The system shall maintain the integrity of the Data Management database through referential integrity.
The system shall allow query requests against all metadata used to manage the repository.
The system shall support the production of management information reports and statistics triggered by a calendar or system event.
The system shall allow online updates to individual records by authorized staff.
The system shall provide a mechanism for deleting AIPs and their associated metadata from the repository.
The system shall control access to data based on multiple permission levels including create, edit, read, and delete privileges.
The system shall restrict access to materials based on licensing terms, embargo periods, and IP range restrictions.
The system shall maintain audit logs of all actions performed by individual users.
The system shall support federated searching across multiple repository sites.
The search interface shall be web-accessible and Section 508 compliant.
The system shall provide metadata and full-text searching using standard boolean and proximity functions.
The system shall allow metadata harvesting by other institutions following OAI-PMH guidelines.
The system shall retrieve AIPs from Archival Storage to generate Dissemination Information Packages (DIPs).
The system shall allow users to save digital content to a hard drive and email documents.
The system shall accept metadata in NLM DTD, Dublin Core, MARC21, MARCXML, ONIX, MODS, EAD, and TEI formats.
The system shall automatically extract technical metadata from TIFF image file headers.
